rules.title: Every participant must be an eligible student; working professionals enrolled in degree programs are not eligible.
Team must include 2-5 registered Devpost members; no solo final submission.
Team must complete the AI Readiness Qualifier and receive an approval code.
Substantial competitive build work must happen during June 14-21, 2026.
Submission must explain AI architecture, human-in-loop design, responsible AI guardrails, tools, and data.
Final Devpost packet must include a working demo or walkthrough and a 3-5 minute pitch video.
WAIBAv playbooks prepare Devpost and qualifier drafts while preserving authorized final-submit gates.

milestones.title: Official rules, track fit, and student gates verified
Product idea, qualifier prep, and evidence model
V product core and synthetic readiness report
Vue demo MVP and submission packet
Qualifier and hackathon ready for eligible student team

evidence.name: student_eligibility_checklist
evidence.private_ref: Student partner must provide private proof

evidence.name: devpost_submission_packet
evidence.private_ref: Devpost account session and qualifier code required

evidence.name: submission_automation
evidence.private_ref: WAIBAv Devpost and qualifier draft workflows

integrations.name: Study-to-work scoring, plan generation, and evidence checks
Interactive student coach demo for judge walkthrough
Qualifier and Devpost draft preparation with final-submit gate
Reusable challenge manifest, scorecard, and evidence renderers

dimensions.label: Problem Understanding
dimensions.evidence: Career readiness, learning path, and portfolio gaps are specific to student users.
dimensions.gap: Replace synthetic personas with the student team lead and two peer interviews.

dimensions.label: AI/Analytics Reasoning
dimensions.evidence: The system uses preference extraction, skill-gap scoring, rubric planning, and explainable recommendations.
dimensions.gap: Add a small evaluation set during build week to compare baseline vs guarded recommendations.

dimensions.label: Solution Design & Architecture
dimensions.evidence: V core, Vue demo, human approval gates, and synthetic-data disclosures.
dimensions.gap: Wire final build-week model call or local rules engine evidence into the demo.

dimensions.label: Impact & Decision Value
dimensions.evidence: The demo converts interests into a week-by-week plan, portfolio artifact, and opportunity match.
dimensions.gap: Capture a before/after student decision log during the hackathon.

dimensions.label: Responsible AI & Ethics
dimensions.evidence: Human-in-loop, bias checks, privacy boundary, over-reliance warnings, and transparent scoring.
dimensions.gap: Student team must confirm final wording and consent policy.

competitors.name: Generic career chatbot
competitors.strength: Easy to understand and fast to demo.
competitors.weakness: Often gives shallow advice without evidence, rubric, or portfolio output.
competitors.counter: Show structured learning-to-work artifacts and human approval gates.

competitors.name: Resume or portfolio generator
competitors.weakness: Starts too late after the student already knows what to build.
competitors.counter: Own the earlier decision: what to learn, why, and what proof to create.

competitors.name: No-code AI workflow
competitors.weakness: May lack architecture clarity and responsible AI substance.
competitors.counter: Pair a simple user experience with inspectable V scoring and data disclosures.

next_moves: Confirm 2-5 eligible students and register every member individually on Devpost before June 6.
Use the qualifier template for one 30-minute team rehearsal before June 7.
During June 14-21, rebuild substantial competitive pieces from the prepared architecture and disclose AI assistance.
Record a 3-5 minute video that shows one student moving from goals to plan, portfolio artifact, and human-approved next step.
Run the WAIBAv draft flow only from an authorized team session with the qualifier code available.

proof_sprint.name: USAII qualifier-to-hackathon proof sprint
proof_sprint.objective: Prepare an eligible student team to qualify, build within the official window, and submit a responsible AI demo with evidence-backed Devpost artifacts.

steps.title: Freeze pre-hackathon package and eligibility gates
steps.output: Rules, package, MVP, and no-private-data evidence

steps.title: Build official demo inside the hackathon window
steps.output: Attributable code, demo, architecture, and responsible AI statement

steps.title: Run Devpost draft automation and submit before 11:59 PM ET

win_conditions: Every team member is an eligible student and individually registered on Devpost.
The team passes the AI Readiness Qualifier and preserves the approval code.
Substantial hackathon work is recreated during June 14-21 with attribution.
Final submission includes architecture, human-in-loop design, responsible AI guardrails, tools/data disclosure, video, and demo.
No private student, school, tax, or age-verification evidence is committed to the public package.

founder_proof.title: Founder and dogfood proof
founder_proof.public_summary: Mexico-based software architecture and automation founder with 15+ years across enterprise integrations, fiscal software, ERP workflows, AI automation, cloud delivery, and technical leadership.
founder_proof.private_boundary: Fiscal IDs, phone, email, CSD keys, legal documents, bank data, and raw OCR records stay private and are referenced only through redacted manifests or checksums.

signals.label: Senior architecture and technical leadership
signals.note: Public CV material supports leadership, architecture, and delivery claims.

signals.label: AI, automation, and Gemini experience
signals.note: Skills file lists AI/LLM integrations, Gemini, scraping, and automation.

signals.label: Fiscal software and enterprise integrations
signals.note: Selected projects include Mexican electronic billing and integration work.

signals.label: Operational profile database
signals.note: Used only to derive public-safe summaries and private evidence boundaries.
